story.title: As a user I want to complete checkout successfully
story.description: On Saucedemo, a user completes the checkout flow: enters first name, last name, and postal code on the information step; the overview page shows the correct items; and finishing the order displays a confirmation message. Checkout component; multi-step E2E journey (cart -> information -> overview -> complete). Fetched from Jira SK-16 (Mode B).

acceptance_criteria: user can enter first name, last name, and postal code

ambiguities.description: The Jira AC is one compound sentence; it has been split into three discrete entries (information entry, overview correctness, finish confirmation). Confirm the split preserves intent.

Checkout has an implicit precondition the AC does not state: at least one product must be in the cart, and the user must be logged in, before the checkout flow can start. The test will add a known item to the cart and log in as setup; this is scaffolding for the AC, not an invented requirement. Confirm this setup is acceptable.

'overview page shows correct items' is interpreted as: the item(s) added to the cart appear on the checkout overview page (same product name(s)). The AC does not mention price/tax totals, so those are treated as out of scope unless you want them included. Confirm this operational definition.

risks.description: Checkout cannot be completed at all (the information step rejects valid input, or Finish does not produce a confirmation), blocking the core purchase flow and all revenue.
The overview page shows the wrong items (missing, extra, or substituted products) versus what the user put in the cart, so the user could confirm an order different from what they intended.
The information step accepts a submission with a missing required field (first name, last name, or postal code) instead of requiring all three, weakening data integrity at checkout.

requirements_reviewed.notes: Gate 1 approved. ACs verbatim from SK-16 (3-way split). All 3 ambiguities accepted: the split, the cart+login setup scaffolding, and 'correct items' = added products appear on overview by name (price/tax totals out of scope).

test_scope_reviewed.notes: Gate 2 approved. 3 TCs, all approved. Two P0 (complete-checkout, correct-overview-items) + one P1 negative (missing-field validation). 100% E2E correct for a multi-page checkout journey; cart+login is shared setup scaffolding.

specs_reviewed.notes: Gate 3 approved. 3 scenarios = 3 approved TCs; full checkout flow walked live (confirmation text, exact overview item, real validation error all verified); login+cart are setup only.

code_reviewed.notes: Gate 4 approved. 3 tests, stable [data-test] locators, web-first assertions, shared beforeEach setup; TC-002 asserts exactly one overview item (catches missing+extra); suite passes live, lint+typecheck clean.
